segments.text: Why do the nations conspire and the peoples plot in vain?
The kings of the earth rise up and the rulers band together against the Lord and against his anointed, saying,
Let us break their chains and throw off their shackles.
segments.speaker: Kings and Rulers

segments.text: Let us break their chains and throw off their shackles.
segments.speaker: Kings and Rulers

segments.text: The One enthroned in heaven laughs; the Lord scoffs at them.
He rebukes them in his anger and terrifies them in his wrath, saying,
I have installed my king on Zion, my holy mountain.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I have installed my king on Zion, my holy mountain.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will proclaim the Lord's decree:
You are my son; today I have become your father.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Ask me, and I will make the nations your inheritance, the ends of the earth your possession.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You will break them with a rod of iron; you will dash them to pieces like pottery.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Therefore, you kings, be wise; be warned, you rulers of the earth.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Serve the Lord with fear and celebrate his rule with trembling.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Kiss his son, or he will be angry and your way will lead to your destruction, for his wrath can flare up in a moment. Blessed are all who take refuge in him.
segments.speaker: Psalmist
